structured.label: Cool fact
structured.value: A circle is perfectly round with no corners at all!

learn.shortText: A circle is a round shape with no sides and no corners. It looks the same all the way around!
learn.detailText: A circle is perfectly round, like a ball, a wheel, or the sun. It has no corners and no straight sides — you can trace all the way around without ever stopping at a corner.

structured.label: Cool fact
structured.value: A square has 4 sides that are all the same size!

learn.shortText: A square has 4 straight sides that are all exactly the same length, and 4 corners.
learn.detailText: A square has 4 straight sides that are all the same length and 4 square corners. You can see squares in windows, crackers, picture frames, and checkerboards.

structured.label: Cool fact
structured.value: A triangle has 3 sides and 3 pointy corners!

learn.shortText: A triangle is a shape with 3 straight sides and 3 corners.
learn.detailText: A triangle has 3 straight sides and 3 pointy corners. You can spot triangles in a slice of pizza, a roof on a house, or a party hat!

structured.label: Cool fact
structured.value: A rectangle has 2 long sides and 2 short sides!

learn.shortText: A rectangle has 4 sides and 4 corners, with 2 long sides and 2 short sides.
learn.detailText: A rectangle has 4 corners and 4 sides — two long ones and two short ones. Doors, books, phones, and beds are all shaped like rectangles.

learn.shortText: A star shape has 5 points sticking out, just like the stars we see twinkling at night.
learn.detailText: A star shape has 5 pointy points. We draw stars to look like the twinkling lights in the night sky, and you'll find them on flags and gold stickers too!

learn.shortText: A heart has two round bumps at the top and a point at the bottom. It's the shape we use to say 'I love you!'
learn.detailText: A heart has two rounded bumps at the top that curve down to a single point at the bottom. People draw hearts to show love, especially on Valentine's Day.

structured.label: Cool fact
structured.value: A diamond is like a square balancing on its corner!

learn.shortText: A diamond has 4 sides and looks like a square tipped to balance on one of its corners.
learn.detailText: A diamond shape has 4 equal sides and sits balancing on a point, like a kite flying in the wind. It's also called a rhombus.

learn.shortText: A pentagon is a shape with 5 straight sides and 5 corners.
learn.detailText: A pentagon has 5 straight sides and 5 corners. If you draw a simple house with a pointy roof, the outline is a pentagon!

learn.shortText: A hexagon is a shape with 6 straight sides and 6 corners.
learn.detailText: A hexagon has 6 straight sides and 6 corners. Bees build their honeycomb out of lots of little hexagons fitted neatly together!

structured.label: Cool fact
structured.value: An oval is like a stretched-out circle!

learn.shortText: An oval is a curvy shape like a circle that has been gently stretched longer.
learn.detailText: An oval looks like a circle that has been stretched out long, with no corners. Eggs, rugby balls, and many mirrors are oval shaped.
